front: Photograph: shows silhouetted pedestrians with long shadows dwarfed by the huge dark rectangular windows of the J.P. Morgan building; landmark of 'straight photography'
Photograph: shows an old woman with one eye closed, wearing a sign reading 'Blind' around her neck; taken with a false lens pointing 90 degrees away from the subject
Photograph: extremely flat composition showing a white fence forming a diagonal line in the foreground before two Port Kent, New York houses
Film: short city-symphony documentary co-directed with Charles Sheeler; intertitles from Walt Whitman poems; pioneered the city-symphony genre
Artist: student of Lewis Hine; interest in photography began when Hine took him to Alfred Stieglitz's 291 Gallery
Artist: his meeting with Ansel Adams inspired Adams to abandon Pictorialism; his close-cropping technique inspired Georgia O'Keeffe's large-scale flower paintings
Artist: worked in Mexico, becoming head of the Education Department's Photo and Film division; photographed Woman of Alvarado, Veracruz
Artist: traveled to Italy with the screenwriter of Bicycle Thieves and photographed an old widow surrounded by her five sons, influenced by neorealism
